lesson.menu_title: The Verb To Be in Action
lesson.menu_desc: Describing yourself, your company, and your role at Sucafina -- 31 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 2 -- The Verb To Be: You, Your Company, Your Role
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Carlos Eduardo | Aula 2 -- The Verb To Be

vocab.title: Match each word to its meaning
defs: a business that makes or sells things
a person who studies information and reports it
in charge of something; you take care of it
to send products to other countries
with its main office in a place
a person or farm that grows a product

paras: Sucafina is a coffee company. It is not a small company — it is big and international. Sucafina is not Brazilian. It is Swiss, and it is headquartered in Switzerland. Carlos is an analyst at Sucafina. He is from Varginha, in Brazil.

Sucafina is a global company. There are teams in many countries. The offices are in Africa, Europe, and America. Carlos is responsible for sustainability. He is not a farmer — he is an analyst. The coffee producers are important partners. They are in southern Minas Gerais.

To describe your company, use the verb 'to be'. With 'I', use 'am': I am an analyst. With 'he', 'she', or 'it', use 'is': Sucafina is Swiss. With 'you', 'we', or 'they', use 'are': The producers are in Brazil. For 'no', add 'not': It is not Brazilian. For a question, put the verb first: Is Sucafina Swiss? Are you from the Nairobi office?
reading.source: Alumni by Better — A1 reading, adapted from a company profile

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Sucafina is a small Brazilian farm owned by Carlos.
Sucafina is a big, international Swiss coffee company, and we use 'to be' to describe it.
The text explains how to grow coffee in Switzerland.

items: The text says: 'It is Swiss, and it is headquartered in Switzerland.'
The text says: 'He is not a farmer — he is an analyst.'
The coffee producers are in southern Minas Gerais.
The text says: 'They are in southern Minas Gerais.'
The text says: 'It is not a small company — it is big and international.'

parts: a Swiss coffee company. The producers

gapfill.text: Remember: I am / he-she-it is / you-we-they are. With names and companies (Carlos, Sucafina), use 'is'. With 'the producers' (plural), use 'are'.

bank.label: Useful sentences to describe your company
items: I am an analyst at Sucafina.
Is Sucafina a big company? Yes, it is.

lf.text: Affirmative: to be comes after the subject (Sucafina IS...). Negative: add 'not' (is not / isn't). Question: put 'to be' FIRST (Are you...?).

items.situation: Say who you are and your job in one sentence.
tips: I am ... and I am an analyst at ...
My name is ... I am a ...

items.situation: Describe your company. Is it Brazilian or Swiss? Big or small?
Make it negative: Sucafina is NOT a Brazilian company.
tips: It is not Brazilian. It is Swiss.

items.situation: Ask a colleague a 'to be' question about their office or country.
tips: Are you from the Nairobi office?

items: Describe Sucafina in three sentences: what it is, where it is headquartered, and what you are responsible for. Use your notes.
Say the same thing again, and add one negative sentence: 'It is not a Brazilian company — it is Swiss.'
Now do it with no notes. Sound natural, not memorized.
A colleague asks: 'Are the producers in Africa?' Answer with a short 'to be' answer: 'No, they aren't. They are in Brazil.'

answerkey.title: Reveal the model description
list: Model: 'Hello. My name is Carlos. I am an analyst at Sucafina. Sucafina is a Swiss coffee company. It is headquartered in Switzerland. It is not Brazilian. The producers are in southern Minas Gerais, and I am responsible for sustainability.'
Affirmative: I am / it is / they are.
Negative: I am not / it is not (isn't) / they are not (aren't).
Question: Am I...? / Is it...? / Are they...?
answerkey.note: With I use am; with he/she/it and singular names use is; with you/we/they and plurals use are.

listenings.text: Hello, and welcome to the company profile. Sucafina is a coffee company. It is not a small business — it is big and international. Sucafina is not Brazilian. It is Swiss, and it is headquartered in Switzerland. There are teams in Africa, Europe, and America. Sucafina works with many coffee producers. In Brazil, the producers are in southern Minas Gerais. Carlos is an analyst there. He is responsible for sustainability. Thank you for listening.

Hi Carlos, this is Emma from the Switzerland office. I have a quick question before the meeting. A new colleague wants to know about your work. Please tell her three things: who you are, what your company is, and what you are responsible for. Keep it short and simple, with the verb 'to be'. For example: I am an analyst, Sucafina is a Swiss company, and I am responsible for sustainability. Thank you, and see you soon.

extra_audio.text: My name is Carlos. I am an analyst at Sucafina. Sucafina is a Swiss coffee company. It is not Brazilian. The producers are in Brazil, and I am responsible for sustainability.
